title: How Computers, Data, and the Internet Work

learning_objectives: Learn what data is and why it matters.
Understand the basic flow of the internet.

lesson.title: Data, Browser, Server, and API Basics

blocks.title: Week 2 Focus
blocks.content: Students learn how information moves from user to browser, server, API, and back to the screen.

items.label: Internet Flow
items.description: See how users, browsers, servers, and APIs connect.

items.label: APIs
items.description: Explain APIs as connectors between systems.

items.label: Browser
items.description: The app that lets a user access websites or web systems.

items.label: Server
items.description: A system that receives requests and sends back results.

items.label: API
items.description: A connector used to exchange information between systems.

items.label: Database
items.description: The place where structured data is stored and retrieved.

blocks.title: Teaching Tip
blocks.content: Use a weather app example so students can visualize request, server processing, and returned data.

activity.title: Internet Role-Play
activity.instructions: Arrange the roles in the correct order for a simple web request.
activity.success_criteria: Place the roles in a logical request-and-response order.

questions.prompt: Which part of a web system usually stores information for later use?
questions.explanation: A database stores structured information so apps can retrieve it later.

questions.prompt: What is the best simple description of an API?
options: A connector that lets systems exchange data
questions.explanation: An API helps one system request or send information to another system.

questions.prompt: Which item is the best example of structured data?
questions.explanation: Spreadsheets with rows and columns are a common example of structured data.

reflection.prompt: Describe what happens when a learner opens a weather app and checks today’s temperature. Mention browser or app, server, and data.
reflection.placeholder: The user opens the app, the app sends a request to a server or API, the server returns data, and the app displays the temperature.

teacher_notes: Act out the user, browser, server, API, and database roles physically if teaching in a classroom.
Use a real JSON response to reduce abstraction.
